text: Generalizes the two-term mechanism (a-0020) from the none-vs-coarse contrast to the full granularity AXIS. Across all 9 (rung x toolset) cells the discount-scaled two-term net predicts the actual billed cost gap vs none with corr 0.921, through-origin slope 0.83, mean abs error $0.33. The cost benefit is MONOTONE in granularity at every rung (fine<=medium<=coarse): tier-1 [-0.56,-0.04,+0.33], tier-2 [+0.30,+1.46,+2.16], tier-3 [-1.14,-0.61,+0.01]. So coarse is not a special case — it is the endpoint of a single granularity gradient that the same externalization-saving-minus-context-tax mechanism explains throughout. Refinement target: the FINE toolset is the worst-fit cell (benefit over-predicted), because its tool-orchestration output is not captured by the in-head-saving proxy — motivates a three-term model. Caveat: single Opus-4.7, 1 puzzle/tier.
